{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|12=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|12=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|12=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|12=draft}}, formerly ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|12=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|12=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|12=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|12=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|12=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:111-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}}

Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|12=draft|12=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|12=draft|12=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=draft|12=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=draft|12=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=draft|12=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:111-19]] [[Category:refreshing imported pages]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}}

The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=draft|12=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=draft|12=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=draft|12=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}}

revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|12=draft|12=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|12=draft|12=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|12=draft|12=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|12=draft|12=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=draft|12=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=draft|12=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=draft|12=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|12=draft|12=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|12=draft|12=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|12=draft|12=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|12=draft|12=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=draft|12=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|12=draft|12=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=draft|12=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=draft|12=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}}

In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=draft|12=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}}

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}}

From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]]

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{DEFAULTSORT:Tsunomine Shrine}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]]

It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]]

{{出典の明記|date=2020年4月}} {{神社 | 名称=津峯神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|260px]]
拝殿 | 所在地=[[徳島県]][[阿南市]]津乃峰町東分343 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=賀志波比売命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[郷社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） | 例祭=[[11月17日]]・[[11月18日|18日]] | 神事=桜祭 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=津峯神社|coordinates= {{coord2|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E}}}} }} [[ファイル:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|サムネイル|[[津乃峰山]]の山頂部
中央に津峯神社の鳥居が見える]] '''津峯神社'''（つのみねじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]]の[[津乃峰山]]山頂にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 漁師や船舶・交通関係者の信仰を集めている。津峯神社、[[大麻比古神社]]（鳴門市）、[[二ノ宮八幡神社]]（名西郡神山町）の例大祭を「阿波の三大祭り」と呼ぶことがある{{Cite book|和書 |author=徳島史学会 |title=徳島県の歴史散歩 |publisher=山川出版社 |year=1976 |page=108}}。 == 祭神 == * 賀志波比売大神（かしはひめのおおかみ） * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]]（おおやまつみのおおかみ） 賀志波比売命を主[[祭神]]とし、相殿に[[大山祇命]]を祀る。開運延命・病気平瘉・海上安全の神として信仰される。賀志波比売大神は主として人の寿命を司り、一日に一人の命を助けるという{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/about.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=津峯神社 |accessdate=2023-01-29}}。 境内社の恵比須大神は「阿波の七福神」の一つ{{Cite book|和書 |title=阿波の神々と祭り（三） |year=2000 |publisher=岡島隆夫 |page=53}}。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）、[[神託]]により、国家鎮護・延命長寿の神として賀志波比賣命を霊山・津乃峰山の山頂に祀ったのに始まると伝える。[[延喜式神名帳]]には「阿波国那賀郡 [[賀志波比売神社]]」と記載されている。[[忌部氏]]が社殿を再建した{{Cite book|和書 |author=徳島史学会 |title=徳島県の歴史散歩 |publisher=山川出版社 |year=1976 |page=188-190}}。津乃峰山には大小の洞窟があり、かつては断食参篭の行場であった。 中世に入って[[細川頼之]]や[[三好長慶]]も深く帰依したが、天正年間に[[長宗我部元親|長曾我部元親]]の阿波侵攻による兵火で焼失した{{Cite book|和書 |title=郷土資料辞典 徳島県 |year=1998 |publisher=人文社 |page=124}}。 阿波国主[[蜂須賀家]]が守護神として崇敬した。歴代蜂須賀家の家運長久を祈願し、富岡城主鹿島家と共に正月に参拝祈願をしていた{{Cite book|和書 |title=改訂徳島県神社誌 |year=2019 |publisher=徳島県神社庁 |page=189}}。 1946年（昭和21年）の[[南海地震]]で倒壊したあと再建された。1950年（昭和25年）、[[神社本庁]]の別表神社に加列された。 == 主要建物 == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|大鳥居]] * 本殿 * 幣殿 * 拝殿 * 末社津峯事代主神社 * 神符授与所 * 斎館 * 社務所 * 祓所 ※この主要建物は改定徳島県神社誌を参照 == 賀志波比売神社 == [[津乃峰山]]の麓の[[阿南市]][[見能林町]]柏野22にも、賀志波比売命を祀る[[賀志波比売神社]]がある。現在は見能方町の[[八幡神社 (阿南市見能林町)|八幡神社]]の境外社とされている。社伝では、賀志波比売命が最初に祀られた地であり、その後、賀志波比売命は人々の希望により津乃峰山山頂に遷座したが、元の鎮座地にも祠が残されたものと伝え、当社が「津峯神社の本宮」であるとしている。 後に近くの見能方八幡神社に合祀された。1952年（昭和27年）、賀志波比売神社の社地の大部分が市に提供されて保育所が建設され、神社の社殿は敷地の北西隅に遷されていた。1988年（昭和63年）に保育所が移転したのに伴い、元の境内が神社に返還され、1990年（平成2年）に新社殿が造営された。 == 主な祭事 == * 1月1日：歳旦祭 * 旧7日：初祭・祈年祭 * 2月11日：紀元祭 * 春分の日：春季皇霊殿遙拝式 * 4月3日：桜まつり ** 延命餅投、捧餅競技（子ども・女子・男子の部） * 4月17日・18日：春季大祭　太々神楽祭 * 4月29日：昭和祭 * 6月30日：茅の輪くぐり・大祓式・悪病災難除祈願祭 * 旧8月15日：観月祭（中秋の名月） * 秋分の日：秋季皇霊殿遙拝式 * 11月：七五三詣り * 11月3日：文化祭 * 11月17日・18日：秋季例大祭 ** 例祭式、阿南相撲大会津峯場所、捧餅競技（子ども・女子・男子の部）、神幸式（御輿渡御）、延命餅投 * 11月23日：新嘗祭 * 12月23日：天長祭 * 12月31日：大祓式・延命長寿厄除開運星祭・除夜祭 ※毎月17日：月並祭 ※この祭事情報は津峯神社ウェブサイトを参照{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/ |title=祭典・行事 |publisher=津峯神社 |accessdate=2023-01-29}}。 === 捧餅競技 === 桜まつりと秋季例大祭では、子ども・女子・男子の部毎に、大三宝に乗せた大鏡餅を持ち上げて土俵を回る捧餅競技が行われている{{Cite book|和書 |title=徳島県百科事典 |year=1981 |publisher=徳島新聞社 |page=629}}。 == 現地情報 == ; 所在地 * [[徳島県]][[阿南市]][[津乃峰町]]東分343 ; 交通アクセス [[ファイル:津峯神社 リフト乗り場.jpg||thumb|参詣リフト乗り場]] * 自動車 ** 8合目までは[[津峯スカイライン]]が通っており、8合目に250台駐車可能な駐車場がある{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/access.html |title=アクセス |publisher=津峯神社 |accessdate=2023-01-29}}。駐車場からは300段程の石段か参詣リフトを利用する。 * 徒歩 ** 登山ルートあり == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist|2}} == 外部リンク == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{神道 横}} {{shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:つのみねしんしや}} [[Category:徳島県の神社]] [[Category:阿波国の式内社]] [[Category:徳島県の旧郷社]] [[Category:別表神社]] [[Category:阿南市の歴史]] [[Category:阿南市の建築物]]

拝殿 | 所在地=[[徳島県]][[阿南市]]津乃峰町東分343 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | 祭神=賀志波比売命 | 社格=[[式内社]]（小）・旧[[郷社]]・[[別表神社]] | 創建=[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]） | 例祭=[[11月17日]]・[[11月18日|18日]] | 神事=桜祭 | 地図={{Location map|Japan Tokushima|label=津峯神社|coordinates= {{coord2|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E}}|position=left|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=280|coord={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E}}}} }} '''津峯神社'''（つのみねじんじゃ）は、[[徳島県]][[阿南市]]の[[津乃峰山]]山頂にある[[神社]]である。[[式内社]]で、旧[[社格]]は[[郷社]]。現在は[[神社本庁]]の[[別表神社]]。 漁師や船舶・交通関係者の信仰を集めている{{Sfn|徳島史学会|1976|pp=188-190}}。津峯神社、[[大麻比古神社]]（鳴門市）、[[二ノ宮八幡神社]]（名西郡神山町）の例大祭を「阿波の三大祭り」と呼ぶことがある{{Sfn|徳島史学会|1976|p=108}}。 津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|[[津乃峰山]]の山頂部
中央に津峯神社の鳥居が見える == 祭神 == * 賀志波比売大神（かしはひめのおおかみ） * [[オオヤマツミ|大山祇大神]]（おおやまつみのおおかみ） 賀志波比売命を主[[祭神]]とし、相殿に[[大山祇命]]を祀る。開運延命・病気平瘉・海上安全の神として信仰される。賀志波比売大神は主として人の寿命を司り、一日に一人の命を助けるという{{Cite web|和書|url=http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/about.html |title=御由緒 |publisher=津峯神社 |accessdate=2023-01-29}}。 境内社の恵比須大神は「阿波の七福神」の一つ{{Sfn|岡島|2000|p=53}}。 == 歴史 == 社伝では、[[神亀]]元年（[[724年]]）、[[神託]]により、国家鎮護・延命長寿の神として賀志波比賣命を霊山・津乃峰山の山頂に祀ったのに始まると伝える。[[延喜式神名帳]]には「阿波国那賀郡 [[賀志波比売神社]]」と記載されている。[[忌部氏]]が社殿を再建した{{Sfn|徳島史学会|1976|pp=188-190}}。津乃峰山には大小の洞窟があり、かつては断食参篭の行場であった。 中世に入って[[細川頼之]]や[[三好長慶]]も深く帰依したが、天正年間に[[長宗我部元親|長曾我部元親]]の阿波侵攻による兵火で焼失した{{Sfn|ゼンリン|1998|p=124}}{{Sfn|徳島史学会|1976|pp=188-190}}。 阿波国主[[蜂須賀家]]が守護神として崇敬した。歴代蜂須賀家の家運長久を祈願し、富岡城主鹿島家と共に正月に参拝祈願をしていた{{Sfn|徳島県神社庁教化委員会|2019|p=189}}。 1946年（昭和21年）の[[南海地震]]で倒壊したあと再建された{{Sfn|ゼンリン|1998|p=124}}{{Sfn|徳島史学会|1976|pp=188-190}}。1950年（昭和25年）、[[神社本庁]]の別表神社に加列された。 {{-}} == 主要建物 == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|大鳥居]] * 本殿 * 幣殿 * 拝殿 * 末社津峯事代主神社 * 神符授与所 * 斎館 * 社務所 * 祓所 ※この主要建物は改定徳島県神社誌を参照{{Sfn|徳島県神社庁教化委員会|2019|p=189}} == 賀志波比売神社 == [[津乃峰山]]の麓の[[阿南市]][[見能林町]]柏野22にも、賀志波比売命を祀る[[賀志波比売神社]]がある。現在は見能方町の[[八幡神社 (阿南市見能林町)|八幡神社]]の境外社とされている。社伝では、賀志波比売命が最初に祀られた地であり、その後、賀志波比売命は人々の希望により津乃峰山山頂に遷座したが、元の鎮座地にも祠が残されたものと伝え、当社が「津峯神社の本宮」であるとしている。 後に近くの見能方八幡神社に合祀された。1952年（昭和27年）、賀志波比売神社の社地の大部分が市に提供されて保育所が建設され、神社の社殿は敷地の北西隅に遷されていた。1988年（昭和63年）に保育所が移転したのに伴い、元の境内が神社に返還され、1990年（平成2年）に新社殿が造営された。 == 主な祭事 == * 1月1日：歳旦祭 * 旧7日：初祭・祈年祭 * 2月11日：紀元祭 * {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinai-sha of Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Q11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]]

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinai-sha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]]

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Q11]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[en:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]]

It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine.
