revision.text:
{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Small)・Gōsha・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival=[[:en:November 17|November 17]]・[[:en:November 18|November 18]] | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] [[Category:History of Anan city]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}}

In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] [[Category:History of Anan city]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}}

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}} (Small)・Gōsha・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival=[[:en:November 17|November 17]]・[[:en:November 18|November 18]] | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 延喜式神名帳}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}}

After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556056]] Wikidata name: Tsunomine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival=[[:en:November 17|November 17]]・[[:en:November 18|November 18]] | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}}

The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Ninomiya hachinan-jinja|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}}

Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556056]] Wikidata name: Tsunomine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{Coord|33.89472|N|134.64361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival=[[:en:November 17|November 17]]・[[:en:November 18|November 18]] | ceremony=Sakura festival | prefecture={{ill|Anan|qid=Q485583}} }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]]

In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] }} [[sn:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[sn:sunomine Shrine]] {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556056]] Wikidata name: Tsunomine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{Coord|33.89472|N|134.64361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival=[[:en:November 17|November 17]]・[[:en:November 18|November 18]] | ceremony=Sakura festival | prefecture={{ill|Anan|qid=Q485583}} }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}}

The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|qq=|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}}

Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|qq=|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. [[sn:commons:Category:Tsunomine-jinja]][[ja:津峯神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11556056]] Wikidata name: Tsunomine-jinja [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:09, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{Coord|33.89472|N|134.64361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・[[:en:Beppyo shrine|Beppyo Shrine]] | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival=[[:en:November 17|November 17]]・[[:en:November 18|November 18]] | ceremony=Sakura festival | prefecture={{ill|Anan|WD=Q485583}} }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in [[:en:Anan, Tokushima|Anan City]], [[:en:Tokushima Prefecture|Tokushima Prefecture]]. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the [[:en:Association of Shinto Shrines|Association of Shinto Shrines]]. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, [[:en:Ōasahiko Shrine|Oasahiko Shrine]] in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}}

The [[:en:Inbe clan|Inbe Clan]] rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as [[:en:Hosokawa Yoriyuki|Hosokawa Yoriyuki]] and [[:en:Miyoshi Nagayoshi|Miyoshi Nagayoshi]], but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by [[:en:Chōsokabe Motochika|Chosokabe Motochika]] in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 [[:en:Nankai earthquakes|Nankai Earthquake]], the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]
