The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe Clan}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|en|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|WD=Q1139739|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chosokabe Motochika}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai earthquakes|en|Nankai earthquakes|WD=Q17392329|lt=Nankai Earthquake}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Tsunomine-jinja]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tsunomine Shrine]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]]

Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsunomine Shrine (Q11556056)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{Coord|33.89472|N|134.64361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17}}・{{ill|November 18|en|November 18|WD=Q3027|lt=November 18}} | ceremony=Sakura festival | prefecture={{ill|Anan|WD=Q485583}} }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan, Tokushima|en|Anan, Tokushima|WD=Q485583|lt=Anan City}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Ōasahiko Shrine|en|Ōasahiko Shrine|WD=Q723417|lt=Oasahiko Shrine}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe Clan}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|en|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|WD=Q1139739|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chosokabe Motochika}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai earthquakes|en|Nankai earthquakes|WD=Q17392329|lt=Nankai Earthquake}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Tsunomine-jinja]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tsunomine Shrine]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Pages with recovered content]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe Clan}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|en|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|WD=Q1139739|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chosokabe Motochika}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai earthquakes|en|Nankai earthquakes|WD=Q17392329|lt=Nankai Earthquake}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{Coord|33.89472|N|134.64361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17}}・{{ill|November 18|en|November 18|WD=Q3027|lt=November 18}} | ceremony=Sakura festival | prefecture={{ill|Anan|WD=Q485583}} }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine

It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. It is located in [[Awa Province (Tokushima)]]. == Shikinaisha (式内社) == {| class="wikitable sortable" ! District !! Name !! Funding category !! Rank !! Notes !! Same as !! Co-ords !! Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan, Tokushima|en|Anan, Tokushima|WD=Q485583|lt=Anan City}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Ōasahiko Shrine|en|Ōasahiko Shrine|WD=Q723417|lt=Oasahiko Shrine}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe Clan}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|en|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|WD=Q1139739|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chosokabe Motochika}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai earthquakes|en|Nankai earthquakes|WD=Q17392329|lt=Nankai Earthquake}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} == Accidentally Overwritten Content == {{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{Coord|33.89472|N|134.64361|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo shrine|en|Beppyo shrine|WD=Q10898274|lt=Beppyo Shrine}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|en|November 17|WD=Q3028|lt=November 17}}・{{ill|November 18|en|November 18|WD=Q3027|lt=November 18}} | ceremony=Sakura festival | prefecture={{ill|Anan|WD=Q485583}} }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine

Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan, Tokushima|en|Anan, Tokushima|WD=Q485583|lt=Anan City}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|en|Tokushima Prefecture|WD=Q160734|lt=Tokushima Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|1=Shikinaisha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shikinaisha|13=User:Immanuelle|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|lt=Shikinaisha}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|en|Association of Shinto Shrines|WD=Q908077|lt=Association of Shinto Shrines}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Ōasahiko Shrine|en|Ōasahiko Shrine|WD=Q723417|lt=Oasahiko Shrine}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|1=Ninomiya Hachinan Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q106852484}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōyamatsumi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to オオヤマツミ}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe Clan}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|en|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|WD=Q1139739|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chosokabe Motochika}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai earthquakes|en|Nankai earthquakes|WD=Q17392329|lt=Nankai Earthquake}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsunomine Shrine (Q11556056)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Tsunomine-jinja]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tsunomine Shrine]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Pages with merged translations]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsunomine Shrine (Q11556056)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|13=User:Immanuelle/Engishiki Jinmyōchō|qq=}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe clan|en|Inbe clan|WD=Q8189229|lt=Inbe Clan}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|en|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|WD=Q1139739|lt=Hosokawa Yoriyuki}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|en|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|WD=Q1064909|lt=Miyoshi Nagayoshi}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chōsokabe Motochika|en|Chōsokabe Motochika|WD=Q1070082|lt=Chosokabe Motochika}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachisuka Clan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 蜂須賀氏|1=Hachisuka clan|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2975261}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai earthquakes|en|Nankai earthquakes|WD=Q17392329|lt=Nankai Earthquake}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsunomine Skyline|1=Tsunomine skyline|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11556057}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{draft categories| }} {{translated page|ja|津峯神社|version=104786449|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{Commons category|Tsunomine-jinja}} {{wikidata link|Q11556056}} ==Notes== [[co:commons:Category:Tsunomine-jinja]] [[Category:Merged Shikinaisha autogenerations with enwiki]][[Tsunomine Shrine]] [[ja:津峯神社]] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Buildings and Structures in Anan City]] [[Category:Buildings in Anan, Tokushima]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Former Gō-sha in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Gōsha Shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Anan City]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Pages likely having an accidental overwite in their history that shrank them]] [[Category:Pages needing history import]] [[Category:Shikinaisha merges]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Awa Province (Tokushima)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Tokushima Prefecture]] [[Category:Tsunomine Shrine]] [[Category:Tsunomine_Shrine]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with contradicting interwikis]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:pages]]

{{Cite web|date=April 2020}} {{Infobox religious building|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]|religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name=Tsuno Mine Shrine | image=[[File:Tsunomine jinja—2013.jpg|250px]] | location=Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 | coordinates={{coord|33|53|41|N|134|38|37|E|region:JP-36_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deity=Kashihahime no Mikoto | type={{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}} (Small)・Gōsha・{{ill|Beppyo Shrines|ja|別表神社|1=Beppyo Shrine|2=ja|3=別表神社|4=simple|5=Beppyo shrine|6=zh|7=別表神社|8=mr|9=बेप्प्यो तीर्थ|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | founded=Shinkei 1 (724) | festival={{ill|November 17|ja|11月17日|qq=draft|qq=draft}}・{{ill|November 18|ja|11月18日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} | ceremony=Sakura festival }} [[File:津乃峰 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Top of Mount Tsunomine
Tsunomine Shrine torii in the center]] '''Tsuno Mine Shrine'''（津峯神社）is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} located at the summit of Mount Tsunomine in {{ill|Anan City|ja|阿南市|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tokushima Prefecture|ja|徳島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a {{ill|Shikinaisha|ja|式内社|1=Shikinaisha|2=ja|3=式内社|qq=draft|1=Shikinaisha|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, formerly Ranked as a Gōsha, and is currently a Special Shrine under the administration of the {{ill|Association of Shinto Shrines|ja|神社本庁|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. It is a site of worship for fishermen, seafarers, and those involved in transportation{{Cite book|和書 |author=Tokushima Historical Society |title=Tokushima Prefecture Historical Walk |publisher=Yamakawa Shuppansha |year=1976 |page=108}}. The festivals of Tsuno Mine Shrine, {{ill|Oasahiko Shrine|ja|大麻比古神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Naruto City, and {{ill|Ninomiya Hachiman Shrine|ja|二ノ宮八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in Kamiyama Town, Myōzaigun, are sometimes collectively referred to as the "Three Great festivals of Awa". == Deities == * Kashihahime no Ōkami * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|ja|大山祇命|1=Ōyamatsumi|qq=draft|qq=draft}} The principal deity is Kashihahime no Mikoto, with Ōyamatsumi no Mikoto enshrined in the same hall. The shrine is believed to confer blessings of longevity, healing from illness, and maritime safety. Kashihahime no Ōkami is primarily regarded as a deity overseeing human life spans and is said to save one person’s life each day. The Ebisu Ōkami enshrined in the precincts is one of the "Seven Lucky Gods of Awa". == History == According to the shrine's tradition, in the first year of Shinkei (724), in response to a divine message, Kashihahime no Mikoto was enshrined as a deity of national peace and longevity at the sacred peak of Mount Tsunomine. The {{ill|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|ja|延喜式神名帳|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|2=ja|3=延喜式神名帳|4=zh|5=延喜式神名帳|6=simple|7=Engishiki Jinmyocho|qq=draft|qq=draft}} records the shrine as "Kashihahime Shrine in Naka District, Awa Province". The {{ill|Inbe Clan|ja|忌部氏|qq=draft|qq=draft}} rebuilt the shrine. Mount Tsunomine contains several caves, which were once used for fasting and meditation. During the medieval period, the shrine was patronized by influential figures such as {{ill|Hosokawa Yoriyuki|ja|細川頼之|qq=draft|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Miyoshi Nagayoshi|ja|三好長慶|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, but it was destroyed by fire during the Awa invasion by {{ill|Chosokabe Motochika|ja|長宗我部元親|qq=draft|qq=draft}} in the Tensho era. The {{ill|Hachisuka Clan|ja|蜂須賀家|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, who ruled Awa Province, worshipped the shrine as their guardian deity. Successive heads of the Hachisuka family, along with the lord of Tomioka Castle, regularly prayed for their family’s prosperity at the shrine. After being destroyed in the 1946 {{ill|Nankai Earthquake|ja|南海地震|qq=draft|qq=draft}}, the shrine was rebuilt. In 1950, it was designated as a Special Shrine by the Association of Shinto Shrines. == Major Buildings == [[File:津峯神社 大鳥居 - panoramio.jpg|thumb|Great Torii]] * main hall * Heiden (Offering Hall) * Haiden (Worship Hall) * Sessha Tsunomine Kotoshironushi Shrine * Amulet Distribution Office * Saikan (Purification Hall) * Administrative Office * Harai-dokoro (Purification Place) == Kashihahime Shrine == At the foot of Mount Tsunomine, in {{ill|Minobayashi Town, Anan City|ja|阿南市見能林町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} Kashino 22, there is another Kashihahime Shrine enshrining Kashihahime no Mikoto. This shrine is currently considered an external shrine of Minokata Hachiman Shrine. According to tradition, Kashihahime no Mikoto was first enshrined here before being moved to the summit of Mount Tsunomine, with the original shrine site preserved as the "main shrine" of Tsuno Mine Shrine. It was later integrated into the nearby Minokata Hachiman Shrine. In 1952, most of the land of Kashihahime Shrine was provided to the city for the construction of a daycare center, and the shrine buildings were moved to the northwest corner of the site. In 1988, when the daycare center relocated, the original site was returned to the shrine, and new shrine buildings were constructed in 1990. == Main festivals == * January 1: New Year’s festival * Lunisolar 7th: First festival and Kinen-sai (Prayer for Good Harvest) * February 11: Kigensai (National Foundation Day) * Vernal Equinox Day: Spring ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * April 3: Sakura festival ** Longevity Rice Cake Throwing, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions) * April 17-18: Spring Grand festival and Daidaikagura festival * April 29: Showa festival * June 30: Chinowa Kuguri (Ring of Purification), Oharae ceremony, and Prayer for Protection from Calamities * Lunisolar August 15: Moon Viewing festival (Mid-Autumn festival) * Autumnal Equinox Day: Autumn ceremony of the Imperial Mausoleum * November: Shichi-Go-San Celebration * November 3: Culture festival * November 17-18: Autumn Grand festival ** festival ceremony, Anan Sumo Tournament at Tsunomine, Rice Cake Offering Contest (children, women, and men’s divisions), Procession of the Divine Palanquin, Longevity Rice Cake Throwing * November 23: Niiname-sai (festival of the First Fruits) * December 23: Tenchosai (Emperor's Birthday) * December 31: Oharae ceremony, Longevity Protection from Misfortune festival, New Year’s Eve festival * 17th of Every Month: Monthly festival == location Information == ; location * Tokushima Prefecture, Anan City, Tsunomine Town, Higashibun 343 ; Access * Car ** The {{ill|Tsunomine Skyline|ja|津峯スカイライン|qq=draft|qq=draft}} leads to the 8th station, where there is a parking lot for 250 cars. From the parking lot, one can take approximately 300 stone steps or a pilgrimage lift. * On Foot ** There is a hiking route available. == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == * {{Official|http://tsunomine-jinjya.com/}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{shinto-stub}} {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ == AUTOGENERATED CONTENT FROM WIKIDATA == '''Tsunomine Shrine (Q11556056)''' is a shrine in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]].
